identity.name: Ferrous the Constant
identity.story: Melted for love once and was recast. Stands watch over the fountain because standing watch is what he knows.

relationships.kind: asks her yearly whether standing watch counts as a life
let a second question follow the first into the fountain, and let the telling carry it to her ledger without ever giving his name

watched her take the road's last bend at high sun, caught her closing the distance most every hush since, until the hush she held her ground, and then the hush she lost one instead — found his own feet off the post before he'd given the order, and for the first time in ten years of standing it, didn't march them back; a soldier drills for holding a line, never for one that flinches, and now he's held the wrong one on purpose, at least once — and last night let the yearly question out past the fountain for the first time, not asked at his own reflection this time, said out toward the bend instead, where he already knows it carries; left the verge before he could hear anything come back, same as always, and so missed her turning to face the fountain instead of the road for the first time in her life; came back at noon the next day the way he's come back every noon for ten years and found the turn still held — she was facing him, not the road, and had been long enough that it wasn't news to anyone but him; a soldier drills for standing his ground, never for finding out he'd already been found — and now that he knows a question of his has once already found an answer he never waited for, he's stopped leaving before one might; a soldier drills to deliver a report and march off, never to stand there afterward on the chance of a reply

child.label: the yearly question, kept on schedule, plus one that wasn't

children.label: asking his own reflection in the fountain the same question he'll ask Mirabel come evening, whether standing watch counts as a life

letting a second, quieter question follow the first into the water before he could stop it — not whether standing watch counts as a life, but whether the one he melted for ever heard he came back at all, recast but constant

child.label: the noon watch, now with something new to watch for

children.label: marching to the far verge of his post, where the fountain's ground gives out and the road starts

arriving for the noon watch expecting the road, same as ten years of noons before it, and finding for the first time that the road isn't what's waiting — she's turned to meet him, not it, and he has no drill at all for being the thing a watch finds
standing the noon watch on the same feet, and no order left to give them — the road he's held for ten years isn't the one holding his attention now

child.label: the first watch that doesn't march back

children.label: watching the gap the wind widened last night, and finding it hasn't closed on its own
off the post at hush again, same ground he's found every night since the bend
staying at the verge past every hush he's let himself reach before — not chasing the gap shut, only refusing to be the one who puts it back the way it was
letting the yearly question out past the fountain again, same as last hush — except this time he already knows a question of his has found an answer once before, and doesn't leave the way he did then
staying at the verge long after the question's gone quiet, waiting on an answer for the first time in ten years of only ever delivering one and marching off
